2603.27012.title: UMI-Underwater: Learning Underwater Manipulation without Underwater Teleoperation
2603.27012.paper_summary: Underwater robotic grasping is difficult due to degraded, highly variable imagery and the expense of collecting diverse underwater demonstrations. We introduce a system that (i) autonomously collects successful underwater grasp demonstrations via a self-supervised data collection pipeline and (ii) transfers grasp knowledge from on-land human demonstrations through a depth-based affordance representation that bridges the on-land-to-underwater domain gap and is robust to lighting and color shift. An affordance model trained on on-land handheld demonstrations is deployed underwater zero-shot via geometric alignment, and an affordance-conditioned diffusion policy is then trained on underwater demonstrations to generate control actions. In pool experiments, our approach improves grasping performance and robustness to background shifts, and enables generalization to objects seen only in on-land data, outperforming RGB-only baselines. Code, videos, and additional results are available at https://umi-under-water.github.io.
2603.27012.paper_summary_zh: 水下機器人抓取因影像退化、高度變化及收集多樣水下示範的高成本而變得困難。我們介紹了一個系統，它 (i) 通過自我監督數據收集管道，自主收集成功的水下抓取示範，並 (ii) 通過基於深度的可供性表示，將抓取知識從陸地人類示範轉移到水下，這種表示橋接了陸地到水下的領域差距，並對光照和顏色變化具有穩健性。訓練於陸地手持示範的可供性模型通過幾何對齊在水下零樣本部署，然後在水下示範上訓練基於可供性的擴散策略以生成控制動作。在泳池實驗中，我們的方法提高了抓取性能和對背景變化的穩健性，並實現了對僅在陸地數據中見過的物體的泛化，超越了僅使用RGB的基準。代碼、視頻和其他結果可在 https://umi-under-water.github.io 獲得。
2603.27012.author: Hao Li et.al.
2603.27012.authors: Hao Li, Long Yin Chung, Jack Goler, Ryan Zhang, Xiaochi Xie, Huy Ha, Shuran Song, Mark Cutkosky
